text: Moving from a crowded entryway into a vibrant, multimedia space, I feel a shift inside me. The first room, awash with the low energy of people milling about, evokes a mild anxiety—anticipation tinged with hesitance. But then, stepping into the colorful, immersive display of R+D, I am struck by a sense of curiosity and playfulness. Surrounded by bold colors, flickering screens, and words about research and innovation, I feel small yet connected, my mind gently awakened. Here, surrounded by symbols of creativity and progress, I sense the philosophical undercurrent of humanity’s restless mind—always observing, always inventing, always becoming. I feel oddly calm, meditative even, as my thoughts drift with the hum of ideas around me.
